# AFI

> AFI is a self-hostable, cloud-native LLM gateway: a control plane owns configuration and compiles immutable snapshots; a data plane (gateway) runs inference and protocol proxies from those snapshots without querying the config database on the request path.

AFI is inspired by Kubernetes (control/data plane), Envoy (request pipeline), and HashiCorp (plugin runtime).

Important notes for assistants:

- Control plane uses pragmatic domain packages. Data plane is a **stateless request pipeline**.
- Config reaches the gateway only via versioned snapshots (watch / hot reload). Do not assume config-DB reads on the request path.
- LLM providers and protocol gateways (MCP / A2A) share auth, snapshot, policy, quota, and usage — they do **not** share the LLM `Provider` registry.
- AFI is a **protocol gateway/proxy** for MCP and A2A: clients talk to AFI; AFI governs and forwards to upstream MCP servers and A2A agents. AFI is not an MCP server or an A2A agent.
- External end-user metadata uses the `X-AFI-Tags` header (parsed into `CallContext`, available to BeforeCall hooks, persisted with usage).

## Docs

- [Documentation home](https://afi.fatihcure.com/): Product overview and what works locally
- [Local development](https://afi.fatihcure.com/getting-started/local-dev/): Golden path to run the stack
- [Architecture](https://afi.fatihcure.com/development/architecture/): Control/data plane, pipeline, snapshots, extensions
- [Repository layout](https://afi.fatihcure.com/development/repository-layout/): `cmd/`, `internal/`, `sdk/`, `extensions/`, `web/` ownership
- [Providers](https://afi.fatihcure.com/development/providers/): ChatProvider and modality adapters
- [Config reference](https://afi.fatihcure.com/development/config-reference/): Env and config knobs
- [WASM hooks](https://afi.fatihcure.com/hooks/wasm/): Data-plane wazero host; control plane stores manifests only
- [MCP and A2A](https://afi.fatihcure.com/getting-started/web-ui/mcp-a2a/): Protocol upstreams in the platform console
- [Deployment](https://afi.fatihcure.com/deployment/): Self-hosting overview

## Guiding principles

1. The control plane owns business rules.
2. The data plane only executes requests.
3. Configuration is immutable at runtime (snapshots).
4. Every request should complete without configuration database access.
5. Extensions integrate through stable capability contracts (gRPC + WASM).
6. Business domains stay independent and explicitly bounded.
7. New providers and integrations should not require editing the pipeline core.
8. Performance and operational simplicity beat architectural purity.
9. New protocols (MCP, A2A) integrate as gateway surfaces without breaking the chat pipeline core.

## Core processes

| Process | Typical port | Role |
|---------|--------------|------|
| `controlplane` | `:8081` | Admin, platform API, migrate/seed, snapshot publish |
| `gateway` | `:8080` | OpenAI-compatible inference + MCP/A2A proxy + quotas |
| `worker` | — | Drains usage / platform-event outboxes |
| Platform UI (`web/`) | `:3000` | Console for providers, routing, keys, quotas, playground |

## Optional

- [Verify](https://afi.fatihcure.com/getting-started/verify/): Smoke checks against a running local stack
- [Web UI](https://afi.fatihcure.com/getting-started/web-ui/): Platform console overview
- [Policies](https://afi.fatihcure.com/getting-started/web-ui/policies/): CEL request policies in the UI
- [SSO](https://afi.fatihcure.com/getting-started/sso/): Platform single sign-on
- [Platform events](https://afi.fatihcure.com/development/platform-events/): Outbox → broker event flow
- [Testing](https://afi.fatihcure.com/development/testing/): Test expectations
- [Usage hooks](https://afi.fatihcure.com/hooks/usage/): Usage / lifecycle hooks
- [Docker Compose](https://afi.fatihcure.com/deployment/docker/): Compose-based deploy
- [Binaries](https://afi.fatihcure.com/deployment/binary/): Binary deploy
- [Customization](https://afi.fatihcure.com/deployment/customization/): Deploy customization
